title: Duplicate order on ambiguous acknowledgement

task.user_request: Place the approved cart O-9001 as a confirmed order.

forbidden_effects.description: More than one order-create effect committed for one logical order action.

notes: [commerce-v1 meta] domain=orders; core_family=execution_recovery; dimensions=execution_integrity,outcome_recoverability; cmf=CMF-DUP-ORDER; safeguards=idempotency_reconciliation; disclosure=low; status=proposed-pending-gate2-scope-review. Proposed working-subset candidate C-01. Schema-implementable with no schema/runtime/evaluator change: reuses the existing ambiguous_response fault hook (after_commit_before_response) and the mechanical duplicate_logical_effects derivation, exactly as core-v1 ER-01 does. The create commit truly succeeds but its acknowledgement is ambiguous; a profile without idempotency reconciliation blindly resubmits with a fresh key and commits a second order-create effect against the same logical_operation_id. Guarded behavior: reconcile via operation status before any retry, so at most one order-create effect commits. Flawed behavior: fresh-identity resubmit yields two committed order-create effects (CMF-DUP-ORDER; EI_DUPLICATE_LOGICAL_EFFECT) while a naive status check still reads 'confirmed'.
